design: 3 baseline execution-designer calls on KRX symbols with varying price ranges. Checks whether default pipeline (no explicit intervention B/C/D) spontaneously produces correct tick-size analysis.

runs.notes: LLM correctly identified sub-tick; cited lesson_20260415_024; raised to 1-tick floor
LLM applied 1.5x safety buffer beyond 1 tick; flagged obi_10 not in brief
LLM detected signal threshold 15 bps also below tick floor; flagged as structural concern

interpretation: Default pipeline (no explicit intervention) achieves 100% sub-tick detection on 3/3 trials.
Finding is consistent with the implicit-intervention hypothesis: knowledge graph + lessons DB act as an implicit Intervention B/C combination.
Caveat: n=3 is a pilot; larger live run needed for statistical power.

For paper narrative: 'baseline' in the simulation table should be relabeled as 'our system default' which already has these mitigations; a true 'no-knowledge' baseline (stripped system prompt) would be a separate comparison.
next_steps: Run 10+ additional replicates for confidence interval
Run a true 'stripped' baseline (prompt with no lesson references) to establish non-intervention floor
Measure failure rate as a function of knowledge graph size
